findings.title: Duplicated Produce Findings boilerplate across 10 scan skills
findings.description: An 18-line 'Compile findings array' instruction block with JSON example template appears identically in all 10 scan-* skills. Each also includes the identical lines 'Always populate snippet with the relevant code lines' and 'Return the findings array to the orchestrator'. This constitutes a repeated instruction block exceeding the 5-line threshold across 2+ skills.
findings.recommendation: Extract the Produce Findings boilerplate into a shared reference document (e.g., references/findings-template.md) and reference it from each scan skill, similar to how scanner-protocol.md is already referenced.

findings.title: Duplicated Scanner Protocol reference across 10 scan skills
findings.description: All 10 scan skills contain a '## Scanner Protocol' section with near-identical text instructing the reader to 'Read and follow the shared scanner protocol at ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/references/scanner-protocol.md'. This 5+ line section (heading, blank line, instructions, bullet list) is repeated across all scanners.
findings.recommendation: This reference pattern is arguably necessary for self-contained skills. Consider whether the code-analyzer agent could inject this reference automatically, removing the need for each skill to declare it.

findings.title: Duplicated Success Checklist pattern across 13 skills
findings.description: 13 of 18 skills include a '## Success Checklist' section with a markdown checkbox list. While the checklist content is skill-specific, the structural pattern and framing text are duplicated. This is a minor structural duplication.
findings.recommendation: The Success Checklist is a useful self-validation tool for each skill. The duplication here is structural rather than content-based. Consider whether a shared template could generate these, but this is low priority.

findings.title: Inconsistent heading hierarchy in scan-quality
findings.description: Steps 1-6 are at H3 level (### Step N) directly under ## Workflow, while Steps 7-15 are at H4 level (#### Step N) nested under H3 'Sub-Section' groupings (Tech Debt, Performance). Step 16 returns to H3. This creates an inconsistent step numbering depth where some steps are peers and others are children.
findings.recommendation: Either promote Steps 7-15 to H3 like Steps 1-6 (removing or repurposing the Sub-Section groupings), or demote Steps 1-6 under their own H3 grouping (e.g., '### Sub-Section: Code Health').

findings.title: Large inline JSON example blocks in Produce Findings sections
findings.description: Each of the 10 scan skills contains a 15-18 line JSON example block inside the Produce Findings section showing a sample finding. These examples are dimension-specific (different ID prefixes, titles, descriptions) but follow the same schema structure. The examples consume significant word count in each skill.
findings.recommendation: Reference the output-schemas.md document for the schema definition and provide only a 3-4 line abbreviated example in each skill, or move full examples to a shared reference with per-dimension variants.
